detaileddescription.para: MP4 format.
VP8/VORBIS data in a WEBM container.
3gpp format.
HEIC data in a HEIF container.
Opus data in a OGG container.
Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.
This enumarates list of all the possibly supported output formats for the muxer across all the releases/Platform.
Use #MLMediaMuxerGetSupportedOutputFormats to query for all supported Output formats on the Platform.
This enumarates list of all the possibly supported output formats for the muxer across all the releases/Platform.
Use #MLMediaMuxerGetSupportedOutputFormats to query for all supported Output formats on the Platform.

memberdef.definition: MLMediaMuxerOutputFormat const char* const size_t* out_mime_list_size
memberdef.name: out_mime_list_size

memberdef.argsstring: ((deprecated("Use standard Android SDK and NDK APIs."))) struct MLMediaMuxerSampleData
memberdef.name: __attribute__

param.type: (deprecated("Use standard Android SDK and NDK APIs."))

briefdescription.para: Data type that encapsulates sample data to be written in to MLMediaMuxer.

detaileddescription.para: Stop muxing.
Writes an encoded sample into the muxer.
Start muxing.
Set and store the geodata (latitude and longitude) in the output file.
Set the orientation hint for output video playback.
Add a track with given format information.
Configure the MLMediaMuxer for a given Output format with output path.
Get a list of all the supported mime-types for a given Media Muxer Output Format on the Platform.
Get a list of all the supported Media Muxer Output Formats on the Platform.
Release the MLMediaMuxer instance.
Create a MLMediaMuxer instance.

parameterdescription.para: Upon successful return will point to handle to the created MLMediaMuxer. Or else, it will point to ML_INVALID_HANDLE.
MLMediaMuxer object creation failed with resource allocation failure.
One of the parameters is invalid.
MLMediaMuxer object was created successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Handle to the MLMediaMuxer instance.
One of the parameters is invalid.
MLMediaMuxer object was released successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.

detaileddescription.para: User doesn't own the memory/buffer returned from this API and MUST NOT be freeing or releasing the out_format_list.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MLMediaMuxer instance.
Pointer to MLMediaMuxerOutputFormat. This should not be NULL.
The size of the returned out_format_list. This should not be NULL.
If one of the parameters are invalid.
Successfully retrieved all the supported Output Formats.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.

detaileddescription.para: User doesn't own the memory/buffer returned from this API and MUST NOT be freeing or releasing the out_mime_list.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MLMediaMuxer instance.
The Output format, to which we would like to know all the supported mime-types. The format should be one returned from #MLMediaMuxerGetSupportedOutputFormats.
Pointer to array of mime-type (char array). This should not be NULL.
The size of the returned out_mime_list. This should not be NULL.
If one of the parameters is invalid.
Successfully retrieved all the supported Output Formats.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Handle to the MLMediaMuxer instance.
The format of the muxed output media file. The format should be one returned from #MLMediaMuxerGetSupportedOutputFormats.
The linux-style path for the output media file. This value cannot be empty or NULL.
Failed to configure the MLMediaMuxer object with resource allocation failure.
One of the parameters is invalid.
MLMediaMuxer object was configured successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
If the format is not supported.

detaileddescription.para: This should be called before #MLMediaMuxerStart() and after #MLMediaMuxerConfigure.
The following media format keys are supported for Audio/Video and Metadata tracks.

sect1.title: | Audio Track | Video Track | Metadata Track |
sect1.para: | MLMediaFormat_Key_Mime | MLMediaFormat_Key_Mime | MLMediaFormat_Key_Mime | | MLMediaFormat_Key_Sample_Rate | MLMediaFormat_Key_Width | | | MLMediaFormat_Key_Channel_Count | MLMediaFormat_Key_Height | | | MLMediaFormat_Key_CSD0 | MLMediaFormat_Key_Bt_Rate | | | MLMediaFormat_Key_CSD1 | MLMediaFormat_Key_Frame_Rate | | | | MLMediaFormat_Key_Color_Range | | | | MLMediaFormat_Key_Color_Standard | | | | MLMediaFormat_Key_Color_Transfer | | | | MLMediaFormat_Key_HDR_Static_Info | | | | MLMediaFormat_Key_CSD0 | |

sect1.title: | | MLMediaFormat_Key_CSD1 | |
sect1.para: #MLMediaMuxerGetSupportedMimes can be used to query for all the supported MLMediaFormat_Key_Mime for a given Output Format.

While MLMediaFormat_Key_Mime is must for all tracks, some of the keys aren't mandatory. For Audio track, MLMediaFormat_Key_SAMPLE_RATE and MLMediaFormat_Key_CHANNEL_COUNT is must. For Video track, MLMediaFormat_Key_WIDTH anb MLMediaFormat_Key_HEIGHT are must.
Requierment of providing csd (Codec Specific Data) through MediaFormat is optional for some audio/video mimes where as it is mandatory for some other ones (for example vorbis).
With mp4 container following audio/video and metadata mime types can be supported.

sect1.title: | Audio Mimes | Video Mimes | Metadata Mimes |
sect1.para: | audio/3gpp | video/3gpp | application/ | | audio/mp4a-latm | video/mp4v-es | image/vnd.android.heic | | audio/amr-wb | video/avc | |

sect1.title: | | video/hevc | |
sect1.para: With Webm container following audio/video mime types can be supported.

sect1.title: | Audio Mimes | Video Mimes |
sect1.para: | audio/vorbis | video/x-vnd.on2.vp8 |

sect1.title: | audio/opus | video/x-vnd.on2.vp9 |
sect1.para: With Ogg container, only supported mime is "audio/opus".
If a mime passed through the MLMediaFormat is not supported, it will return MLMediaResult_Unsupported.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MLMediaMuxer instance.
MLHandle to the MLMediaFormat that contains the track's format.
Upon successful return, this will conatin the index of the track added.
One of the parameters is invalid.
If the operation completes successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
The operation is invalid.
If one of the keys in the format is not supported.

sect1.para: This should be called before #MLMediaMuxerStart() and after #MLMediaMuxerConfigure.

Calling this method will not rotate the video frame when muxer is generating the file, but add a composition matrix containing the rotation angle in the output video if the output format is MLMediaMuxerOutputFormat_MPEG4 so that a video player can choose the proper orientation for playback.
Note that some video players may choose to ignore the composition matrix in a video during playback. By default, the rotation degree is 0.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MLMediaMuxer instance.
The rotation degrees. It has to be either 0, 90, 180 or 270.
One of the parameters is invalid.
If the operation completes successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
The operation is invalid.

sect1.para: This should be called before #MLMediaMuxerStart() and after #MLMediaMuxerConfigure.
The geodata is stored in udta box if the output format is MLMediaMuxerOutputFormat_MPEG4, and is ignored for other output formats. The geodata is stored according to ISO-6709 standard.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MLMediaMuxer instance.
The latitude in degree. Its value must be in the range [-90, 90].
The longitude in degree. Its value must be in the range [-180, 180].
One of the parameters is invalid.
If the operation completes successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
The operation is invalid.

sect1.para: Make sure all the tracks have been added (#MLMediaMuxerAddTrack) before calling this.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MLMediaMuxer instance.
One of the parameters is invalid.
If the operation completes successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
The operation is invalid.

sect1.para: The application needs to make sure that the samples are written into the right tracks. Also, it needs to make sure the samples for each track are written in chronological order (e.g. in the order they are provided by the encoder).

For MPEG4 media format, the duration of the last sample in a track can be set by passing an additional empty buffer) with MLMediaCodecBufferFlag_EOS flag and a suitable presentation timestamp set in time_us (of MLMediaMuxerSampleData structure) as the last sample of that track. This last sample's presentation timestamp shall be a sum of the presentation timestamp and the duration preferred for the original last sample. If no explicit END_OF_STREAM sample was passed, then the duration of the last sample would be the same as that of the sample before that.
The buffer can be reused once this method returns.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MLMediaMuxer instance.
The sample data to be written encapsulated in MLMediaMuxerSampleData.
One of the parameters is invalid.
If the operation completes successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
The operation is invalid.

sect1.para: Once the muxer stops, it can not be restarted.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MLMediaMuxer instance.
One of the parameters is invalid.
If the operation completes successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
The operation is invalid.

inbodydescription.para: The track index number, to which to write the sample data into. This should be one of the number returned by #MLMediaMuxerAddTrack.
The buffer's time stamp in microseconds.

The flag about the data. The following flags are supported: MLMediaCodecBufferFlag_KeyFrame: if its a sync/key frame. MLMediaCodecBufferFlag_EOS: To notify end of stream. MLMediaCodecBufferFlag_MuxerData: if it is muxer data (only Exif data block is supported).

parameterdescription.para: SampleData defined by MLMediaMuxerSampleData that needs to be initialized.
